text:
skilled physical therapy. 

Range of motion exercises constitute skilled physical therapy only if they are part of an 
active treatment for a specific disease state, illness, or injury that has resulted in a loss or 
restriction of mobility (as evidenced by physical therapy notes showing the degree of 
motion lost and the degree to be restored). Unskilled individuals may provide range of 
motion exercises unrelated to the restoration of a specific loss of function often safely and 
effectively.
